what_it_does: Reads a design register (register.json) and the mockups directory, then generates an index.html gallery whose card statuses are derived from which mockup files actually exist.
observations: Generates a complete HTML page with inline CSS for a design corpus gallery.
Groups views by area and shows grounding counts.
Determines status as grounded, planned, or build-required based on file existence and a register flag.
Renders cards as linked anchors only when a matching mockup file exists.
Uses placeholder thumbnails when a referenced thumb file is missing.
Adds platform badges (mobile, desktop, responsive, tablet) and a status pill per card.
Writes output to the parent design directory as index.html.
Includes a self-maintenance instruction in the docstring to re-run after mockup changes.
standouts: Status is derived from filesystem reality, not hand-maintained.
Generated gallery is explicitly designed to be un-drifting.
Inline CSS references external CSS variables from design-system.css without defining them here.
conventions: Self-maintenance script convention: re-run after producing or addressing a mockup.
Data-driven rendering from register.json with reality validation.
HTML escaping helper for safe output generation.
Relative path computation based on the script's own location.
concerns: No error handling for missing register.json or malformed JSON.
File handles opened without context managers.
Assumes register keys (areas, views, produced, wave1) exist without validation.
CSS class names reference custom properties (--cache, --kind-present, --kind-model, --await) not defined in this file.
Only checks wave1 under produced; other waves are ignored.
os.listdir on mockups does not distinguish files from directories.
notes: A self-refreshing HTML gallery generator that ties register metadata to the actual mockup files on disk.
questions: What is the exact schema of register.json for areas, views, and produced entries?
Are there waves other than wave1, and should they also be considered?
Where is design-system.css located and what CSS variables does it define?
How are platform badge colors intended to map to the referenced CSS variables?
What does build_required signify versus planned?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it generates index.html from register.json plus existing mockups with status derived from reality, and the code does exactly that.
intention_signals: To keep a design gallery automatically synchronized with the actual set of produced mockups so that status cannot drift from reality.
novelty: The status of each design view is derived from whether its mockup file actually exists, making the gallery a live reflection of the file inventory rather than a manually maintained list.
contribution: A deterministic HTML gallery generator that consumes a register and filesystem state to produce a grounded, navigable index of design mockups.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that reads a design register and the mockups directory to generate an index.html gallery whose card statuses are derived from which mockup files actually exist.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a self-maintaining, reality-grounded HTML gallery of design mockups that stays synchronized with the actual file inventory.

symbols.does: HTML-escapes a value for safe inclusion in generated HTML.
symbols.description: A small helper that coerces its input to a string and applies html.escape, used throughout render_gallery to safely embed register data into HTML attributes and text.

symbols.does: Builds the full HTML gallery string from a register dict and a set of existing mockup paths.
symbols.description: Groups views by area, computes grounded/planned/build-required status by checking whether produced mockup files exist in the provided set, renders cards with thumbnails or placeholders, platform badges, status pills, and links, and returns a complete HTML document string.

symbols.does: Orchestrates loading the register, listing mockups, rendering the gallery, and writing index.html.
symbols.description: Computes paths relative to the script location, loads register.json, collects existing mockup filenames under the mockups/ prefix, calls render_gallery, writes the resulting HTML to the parent design directory as index.html, and prints a short summary.
